ACC.summary: The ACC is a Power Four league that carries 11.05% of national title equity across 1000 simulations. Members fill 11.35% of all playoff field slots — a meaningful slice of the 12-team bracket. Miami Hurricanes is a heavy favorite at 34.7% conference title odds. The next tier includes Louisville Cardinals (11.4%), SMU Mustangs (11.3%), Clemson Tigers (9.1%). Miami Hurricanes averages 9.59 wins and reaches the playoff field in 52.3% of simulations. Miami Hurricanes is the league's top national title threat at 6.27% championship odds, and the top three teams hold 77.7% of the conference's title equity. 3 programs carry at least 1% national title equity, led by Miami Hurricanes (6.3%), Louisville Cardinals (1.2%), SMU Mustangs (1.1%). Schedules are demanding: member teams average +0.6 strength of schedule in the model. Stanford Cardinal brings up the rear at 3.93 average wins.

The next tier includes South Florida (14.2%), East Carolina (13.4%), Tulane Green (12.7%). Navy Midshipmen averages 7.56 wins and reaches the playoff field in 2.9% of simulations. South Florida is the league's top national title threat at 0.02% championship odds. That 5.0% playoff rate is what makes the American a live Group of 6 autobid path in this model.
